goal: Correct the lang toggle to path-based (the site uses /th/ baked mirrors); fixes mixed-locale + TH persistence properly
root_cause: Discovered every page has a baked /th/ mirror and the bottom-nav is path-prefixed — locale is PATH-based, not client-i18n. My earlier fix (saved-lang > path for currentLang + a client-re-render pill) produced a MIXED page on /th/-mirror pages (Thai JS bits + English static board copy) and didn't fix navigation persistence.
fix: initShell currentLang now follows the PATH (explicit ?lang > /th/ path > en; dropped saved-lang priority). The global lang pill now NAVIGATES via computeLanguageHref(/ ↔ /th/) instead of client re-render, and marks the current locale active. So toggling TH lands on /th/<page> (full baked Thai) and the path-prefixed bottom-nav keeps you in /th/ across navigation — the real fix for 'switch to TH → next page → EN'.
verified: harness: /full.html EN active + 'Choose ten cards'; click TH → navigates to /th/full.html, title 'เลือกสิบใบ', TH active, bottom-nav hrefs all /th/. node --check passes; md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
notes: Cross-session bookmark of bare / still loads EN (no auto-redirect — avoided redirect-loop risk on live); the always-present pill covers switching. Supersedes the client-pill approach in 2026-06-18-lang-toggle-persist.
